key_points: Safety concerns about off-campus living raised repeatedly by students
Students ask about safe apartment options near downtown campus
On-campus vs. off-campus trade-offs unclear from official materials
key_quotes: The only question I have is living off campus safer? I love GSU but my only concern is safety.. and nobody talks about this in their videos.
Could you make recommendations about off-campus apartments? Where to live if safety is on the top of my list?

key_points: Weak school spirit — GSU is not a traditional college-experience school
Urban Atlanta vibe replaces campus social scene; not ideal for everyone
Incoming students nervous about cold, non-friendly downtown social culture
key_quotes: The lack of school spirit sounds interesting me. I am a school spirit person, so we'll see how I do.
I'm naturally a nice person and I like making new friends, so hearing that makes me kinda nervous for going downtown.
In Atlanta it's like California where it's weird to be friendly.

key_points: Almost no student data available on dining — a notable silence
Urban campus means students likely eat off-campus frequently
No specific dining hall complaints or praise surfaced in data
key_quotes: GSU is a great school professor and student wise, and location too. There's lots of events going on all the time too.
I studied at GSU in the IEP program. I really enjoyed the university. I loved the buildings and also i loved atlanta.

key_points: No direct student mentions of counseling or mental health resources
Commuter-heavy culture may isolate students who need support networks
Safety anxiety is a recurring background stressor for students
key_quotes: The only question I have is living off campus safer? I love GSU but my only concern is safety.. and nobody talks about this in their videos.
I'm trans and plan on transitioning when I go to college, in your honest opinion do you think that I will have a safe experience there?

key_points: Atlanta Scholarship (~$16,700/yr) makes GSU unusually affordable for internationals
Most international students get the scholarship automatically if applying EA
Even with scholarship, students face ~$8,500/yr deficit — plan accordingly
key_quotes: Georgia State University with its Campus Atlanta Scholarship (which basically every intl gets if they apply EA??)
GSU is offering me a lot of opportunities like a scholarship and admission into its Honors College.
Tuition: $25,272/year. 100% Atlanta Scholarship: $16,706/year. Deficit: ~$8,500/year.

key_points: CS program flagged as viable but not prestigious — internships matter more
Honors College available and praised as an academic differentiator
Atlanta location seen as academic asset for CS internship access
key_quotes: Georgia State is more urban. It'd probably be easier to travel to and have more close internship opportunities.
GSU is a great school professor and student wise, and location too.
I got in as a cis major too and what do you plan on going into after you get your degree and are there a lot of job options for this major?

key_points: Credit transfer issues are a real problem — only 26 of 61 hours transferred for one student
Summer Success Program admission pathway confuses accepted students
Administrative communication gaps leave students with unanswered questions
key_quotes: I got accepted but through the summer success program, do you have insight on what that is or what it means?
Only 26 of 61 credit hours from my A.S. transfer to the college my heart was set on.
If there is anything you would like me to film at GSU or anything you would like me to talk about in a video, let me know!

key_points: Downtown Atlanta location is a genuine asset — internships, culture, diversity
Atlanta traffic is brutal; getting across the city takes up to an hour
Safety in downtown is a real, repeated concern students don't ignore
key_quotes: Don't go out at night. Anyways! My brother goes to college there! There's an ikea, an aquarium, a lot of great stuff! Wouldn't recommend living there though.. not very safe lol.
It does take about an hour to get from one part to another only with traffic.
Paying to park is real!!!

key_points: Atlanta's tech and business ecosystem gives GSU students real internship edges
CS students specifically cite proximity to corporate Atlanta as a differentiator
No strong alumni network data surfaced — outcomes depend on self-initiative
key_quotes: Georgia State is more urban. It'd probably be easier to travel to and have more close internship opportunities. It's more culturally diverse.
Are there a lot of job options for this major?
Atlanta is the preferable location for most though.

key_points: Best value play for international CS students in the Southeast with EA scholarship
Honors College + Atlanta Scholarship combo is genuinely hard to beat cost-wise
Urban campus means no traditional college amenities — you pay for access, not experience
key_quotes: Georgia State University with its Campus Atlanta Scholarship (which basically every intl gets if they apply EA??)
GSU is offering me a lot of opportunities like a scholarship and admission into its Honors College.
I academically think Washington State [is better], but Atlanta is the preferable location.

key_points: Commuter school energy — don't expect a traditional residential campus feel
Diverse, urban, serious student body; less party culture, more hustle culture
Students who thrive here are self-motivated and city-comfortable
key_quotes: The lack of school spirit sounds interesting me. I am a school spirit person, so we'll see how I do.
I'm from atlanta and i want to go here but also want to go to a college out of georgia but also i hate being away from home.
GSU is a great school professor and student wise, and location too. There's lots of events going on all the time too.

key_points: **Safety**: Multiple students cite downtown crime as their top unresolved concern
**Social isolation**: Commuter culture and cold urban vibe leaves newcomers friendless fast
**Transfer credits**: Students routinely lose significant credit hours transferring to GSU
key_quotes: The only question I have is living off campus safer? I love GSU but my only concern is safety.. and nobody talks about this in their videos.
Don't go out at night... Wouldn't recommend living there though.. not very safe lol.
Only 26 of 61 credit hours from my A.S. transfer to the college my heart was set on.

key_points: Atlanta Scholarship auto-awarded to EA international applicants — almost no one knows
Honors College admission bundled with scholarships is an underrated academic track
Downtown campus puts students inside Atlanta's actual job market from day one
key_quotes: Georgia State University with its Campus Atlanta Scholarship (which basically every intl gets if they apply EA??)
GSU is offering me a lot of opportunities like a scholarship and admission into its Honors College.
It'd probably be easier to travel to and have more close internship opportunities. It's more culturally diverse.
